2026-04-08T02:07:43.6593444Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-08T02:05:58.374332349Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":57910,\"title\":\"[Tools] Docs: Azure.GeneratorAgent CHANGELOG and README gaps after MCP Server refactor\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.GeneratorAgent`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #57134 (commit `82d240a1b`) by `@radhgupta`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #57134 refactored `Azure.GeneratorAgent` from a CLI tool with `generate`/`migrate` commands into an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server + tools + skill architecture. Key additions:\\n- `--mcp-server` mode serving 19 auto-discovered tools over stdio\\n- `DeterministicFixRegistry` with 27 error-pattern-to-tool rules\\n- 19 individual MCP tools (regex fixes, nullable annotations, code gen, finalization, etc.)\\n- Skill-driven workflow via `.github/skills/sdk-migration/SKILL.md`\\n- 93 new NUnit tests (243 total)\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- **CHANGELOG.md describes the old CLI architecture**, not the new MCP server architecture. It says \\u0026#34;Added `generate` command\\u0026#34; and \\u0026#34;Added `migrate` command\\u0026#34; — neither of these commands exist in the refactored codebase; the entry point is now `McpServerHost.RunAsync` serving tools over stdio.\\n- **README \\u0026#34;Getting started\\u0026#34; section has no MCP server invocation instructions** — it shows only `dotnet build` but not how to run the server (`dotnet run -- --mcp-server`), how to register it in VS Code / Claude Desktop `settings.json`, or how to configure it for Copilot.\\n- **README \\u0026#34;Key concepts\\u0026#34; tool count is inconsistent**: says \\u0026#34;18 individual tools\\u0026#34; in prose but the table lists only 16; the `src/Mcp/Tools/` directory contains 19 tool files. Three tools are absent from the table: `DiscoverProjectTool`, `GeneratedSnapshotTool`, and `AddCodeGenSuppressTool`.\\n- **README has no \\u0026#34;Authenticate the client\\u0026#34; section** (or explicit note that no auth is required), which is required by the standard README template.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n#### File 1: `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/CHANGELOG.md` — **Edit**\\n\\nReplace the entire body of the `1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)` entry with accurate content reflecting the MCP refactor:\\n\\n```markdown\\n# Release History\\n\\n## 1.0.0-beta.1 (Unreleased)\\n\\n### Features Added\\n\\n- Initial release of `Azure.GeneratorAgent` as an MCP (Model Context Protocol) server for automating Azure SDK code generation and migration workflows.\\n- Added `--mcp-server` mode that exposes 19 auto-discovered deterministic fix tools over stdio transport.\\n- Added `DeterministicFixRegistry` with 27 rules that map build error codes and message patterns to specific tool invocations, covering field renames, type-pattern replacements, missing/obsolete using directives, nullable annotation fixes, and method call replacements.\\n- Added MCP tools: `regex_replacement`, `add_using_directive`, `remove_using_directive`, `nullable_annotation_fix`, `batch_fix`, `build_and_classify`, `classify_errors`, `run_code_generation`, `validate_tsp_config`, `commit_iteration`, `pregen_cleanup`, `migrate_test_samples`, `finalize_migration`, `run_tests`, `rename_codegen_type`, `fetch_to_fromlro`, `discover_project`, `generated_snapshot`, `add_code_gen_suppress`.\\n- Each tool supports both MCP (JSON over stdio) and in-process invocation for testing.\\n- Skill-driven workflow: the [`sdk-migration`](https://github.com/Azure/azure-sdk-for-net/blob/main/.github/skills/sdk-migration/SKILL.md) skill doc drives the full migration pipeline by calling MCP tools directly.\\n\\n### Breaking Changes\\n\\n- Initial release, no breaking changes.\\n\\n### Bugs Fixed\\n\\n- Initial release, no bugs fixed.\\n\\n### Other Changes\\n\\n- Added 243 NUnit tests covering all MCP tools, the deterministic fix registry, build output parsing, and error classification.\\n```\\n\\n#### File 2: `sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/README.md` — **Edit**\\n\\n**A. Fix tool count and add missing tools to the MCP Tools table.**\\n\\nReplace:\\n```\\nEach tool supports both MCP (JSON) and in-process invocation.\\n```\\nwith:\\n```\\nEach tool supports both MCP (JSON) and in-process invocation.\\n```\\n(no change to sentence, but fix number in prose: change \\u0026#34;18 individual tools\\u0026#34; → \\u0026#34;19 individual tools\\u0026#34;)\\n\\nAdd the three missing rows to the MCP Tools table:\\n\\n```markdown\\n| `discover_project` | Discover the `.csproj` and output path for a given service directory |\\n| `generated_snapshot` | Capture a snapshot of generated files before migration for diff comparison |\\n| `add_code_gen_suppress` | Add `[CodeGenSuppress]` attributes to suppress specific generated members |\\n```\\n\\n**B. Add MCP server startup and registration instructions to the \\u0026#34;Getting started\\u0026#34; section.**\\n\\nAfter the existing `dotnet build` block, add a new subsection:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Run as MCP server\\n\\nStart the server over stdio (used by Copilot and other MCP clients):\\n\\n```\\ndotnet run --project sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent -- --mcp-server\\n```\\n\\n#### Register in VS Code (Copilot)\\n\\nAdd to `.vscode/mcp.json` (or your user-level `settings.json` under `\\u0026#34;mcp\\u0026#34;` → `\\u0026#34;servers\\u0026#34;`):\\n\\n```\\n{\\n  \\u0026#34;servers\\u0026#34;: {\\n    \\u0026#34;generator-agent\\u0026#34;: {\\n      \\u0026#34;type\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;stdio\\u0026#34;,\\n      \\u0026#34;command\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;dotnet\\u0026#34;,\\n      \\u0026#34;args\\u0026#34;: [\\n        \\u0026#34;run\\u0026#34;,\\n        \\u0026#34;--project\\u0026#34;,\\n        \\u0026#34;\\\\$\\\\{workspaceFolder}/sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent\\u0026#34;,\\n        \\u0026#34;--\\u0026#34;,\\n        \\u0026#34;--mcp-server\\u0026#34;\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n#### Register in Claude Desktop\\n\\nAdd to `~/Library/Application Support/Claude/claude_desktop_config.json` (macOS) or `%APPDATA%\\\\Claude\\\\claude_desktop_config.json` (Windows):\\n\\n```\\n{\\n  \\u0026#34;mcpServers\\u0026#34;: {\\n    \\u0026#34;generator-agent\\u0026#34;: {\\n      \\u0026#34;command\\u0026#34;: \\u0026#34;dotnet\\u0026#34;,\\n      \\u0026#34;args\\u0026#34;: [\\n        \\u0026#34;run\\u0026#34;,\\n        \\u0026#34;--project\\u0026#34;,\\n        \\u0026#34;C:\\\\\\\\git\\\\\\\\azure-sdk-for-net\\\\\\\\sdk\\\\\\\\tools\\\\\\\\Azure.GeneratorAgent\\u0026#34;,\\n        \\u0026#34;--\\u0026#34;,\\n        \\u0026#34;--mcp-server\\u0026#34;\\n      ]\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n\\n### Authenticate the client\\n\\nNo authentication is required. The MCP server operates entirely on the local file system and does not call any Azure service endpoints.\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo test-backed snippets are required for this change. The Examples section already contains illustrative shell/JSON blocks that do not need to be injected from test files.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nAfter the edits, the README must contain these sections in order. Check each:\\n\\n1. **Getting started** — ✅ present; add the new \\u0026#34;Run as MCP server\\u0026#34; and \\u0026#34;Authenticate the client\\u0026#34; subsections (see Step 1B above).\\n2. **Key concepts** — ✅ present; fix tool count from \\u0026#34;18\\u0026#34; → \\u0026#34;19\\u0026#34; and add three missing rows to the table.\\n3. **Examples** — ✅ present.\\n4. **Troubleshooting** — ✅ present.\\n5. **Next steps** — ✅ present.\\n6. **Contributing** — ✅ present.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `dotnet format sdk/tools/Azure.GeneratorAgent/`\\n\\nNo snippet injection (`Update-Snippets.ps1`) or API export (`Export-API.ps1`) is needed since this is an internal tool, not a public NuGet client library.\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt;
